The [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] had a '''veranda''' as part of its upper structure of the [[Temple Spire/Legends|Tranquility Spire]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Located within the three wings near the pinnacle of the spire, this area housed some of the most potent group meditation chambers in the entire Temple. Along with meditation chambers, the veranda housed sparring chambers used for the instruction of privileged younglings and halls used for special occasions.

==History==
[[File:Yoda_teaching.png|thumb|left|300px|Yoda instructs Jedi younglings in one of the veranda training rooms.]]
Constructed as a part of the Temple's central spire, the veranda was complete as of [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] and serviced the needs of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for the next millennia.<ref name="SWCL" />

During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] brought his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], to the veranda for training with [[Remote/Legends|remotes]]. During the training session, the visiting [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] stood and watched with interest at Skywalker's proficiency with the blade.<ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force real-life book">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Jedi Kenobi woke up early and began his morning [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]] on the grand balcony. Unable to find clarity he sought out [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], finding him in the midst of instructing lightsaber practice on the veranda. Politely interrupting the lesson, Kenobi greeted the [[Bear Clan]] before asking Master Yoda about the missing [[Star system/Legends|star system]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. Using a [[hologlobe]], Kenobi accessed a map reader in one of the veranda's small antechambers and displayed the section of space that Kamino should appear. After posing the quandary to the Bear Clan, the older Jedi deduced that the information must have been erased from the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Temple Archives]].<ref name="AOTCn" />

[[File:Anakin_training_EGtF.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] practicing with [[Remote/Legends|remotes]].]]

At the end of the Clone Wars Palpatine, the Supreme Chancellor of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], was revealed to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] to be none other then [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] Darth Sidious. While members of the Council moved to [[Showdown on Coruscant|arrest]] him, the attempt was in vain and the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] was sent to siege the Temple. Under [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]], the Temple was to be laid waste and its occupants killed on the crime of [[Treason/Legends|treason]]. The [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] stormed the halls of the Temple killing all within. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], leading the onslaught from the front, made his way up to the veranda and slaughtered the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|younglings]] hiding there. As their minder, a blond [[Unidentified Zabrak Jedi (Jedi Temple)|Zabrak Jedi]], lay dying he decried Vader as a traitor and whispered that traitors only betray themselves before the Sith Lord killed him.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion: The Ahakista Gambit">''[[Star Wars: Rebellion: The Ahakista Gambit]]''</ref>

For the next several decades the Temple would be occupied by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], its interior chambers crumbling and gathering dust from disuse. It wasn't until [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] that the veranda was the scene of another slaughter when the secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], made his way from the main entrance hall up to the veranda. Killing the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] stationed on the veranda, Starkiller used the veranda to reach the [[Jedi Trials chamber|Jedi Trials Chamber]], Archives, and [[High Council Tower/Legends|High Council Tower]] during three of his trips to the abandoned edifice.<ref name="TFU">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

[[File:JediTrainingVeranda-TAG.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Vader slays the younglings hiding in one of the small chambers off the veranda.]]

Throughout the rest of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Temple did not have many visitors as the Empire struggled with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. However, following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and the deaths of Sidious and Vader, the Empire lost balance and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] drove the Imperials from the galactic capital. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], recently deemed a Knight by the deceased Yoda reformed the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] and eventually led an recovery mission to the crumbling Temple. Following the utter ruin of the Temple during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the newly formed [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] reclaimed Coruscant and [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Cal Omas]] donated a [[New Jedi Temple|new Temple]] to the Jedi Order for their sacrifices during the war. While the Temple was rebuilt to old specifications, it is unknown if the veranda was included or remodeled.<ref name="Dark Nest I: The Joiner King">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref>

==Layout==
[[File:Temple_corridor.png|thumb|right|200px|[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] walks across the veranda in search of Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]].]]
A large open space within the winged section of [[Temple Spire/Legends|Tranquillity Spire]], the veranda was carpeted in blue which covered the golden stone blocks which made up the floor. A large staircase led out into the wings of the spire where light poured through the [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] ceiling. The veranda walls were lined with balcony areas with wide sweeping staircases leading to private chambers and rooms used for special occasions or ceremonies. Each staircase was flanked by tall [[Bronzium/Legends|bronzium]] statues of ancient masters while a doorway between the stairs led into a small chamber in which meditation and lightsaber training was encouraged. These small, windowed rooms were equipped with a map reader, capable of displaying maps of the galaxy.<ref name="AOTCn" />

The veranda served as a hub of activity for the Spire; granting access to the [[Hall of Knighthood/Legends|Hall of Knighthood]] and the [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolifts]] which ran all the way down to the Grand Balcony and the passages to the four Council Towers as well as the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] Chamber.<ref name="TFU" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Jedi Temple Veranda first appears in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], during a scene in which Obi-Wan Kenobi seeks guidance from Yoda in regards to the missing information on Kamino. While the specific location of the veranda is not directly indicated, the peaks of the Council spires are clearly seen through the windows, indicating it to be high within the Temple's central tower. Supplementary notes to [[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] indicate that the veranda is illustrated as having been located beneath the [[Hall of Knighthood/Legends|Hall of Knighthood]].
